artifacts.sourceTitle: Universidad de Concepcion presenta Programa [genIA] que promueve un uso inclusivo y responsable de la Inteligencia Artificial

artifacts.claimText: UdeC's [genIA] ethics page presents institutional guidelines and interactive tools for deciding how to incorporate generative AI in teaching and research while safeguarding transparency, authorship, privacy, and equity.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Esta sección reúne lineamientos y herramientas interactivas para decidir cómo incorporar IA generativa en docencia e investigación, resguardando transparencia, autoría, privacidad y equidad.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: This section brings together guidelines and interactive tools for deciding how to incorporate generative AI in teaching and research while safeguarding transparency, authorship, privacy and equity.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Page introduction under 'Etica para usar IA con criterio humano'
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a claim about the official [genIA] guide page; it does not assert a binding disciplinary rule.

artifacts.claimText: The [genIA] ethics guide says practical AI use should clearly define what is allowed, ask for declaration and citation of tool use when appropriate, verify student understanding and original contribution, and avoid entering sensitive information into external services.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Definir con claridad qué se permite y qué no. Pedir declaración de uso y citar herramientas cuando corresponda. Verificar comprensión y aporte propio del estudiante. Evitar ingresar información sensible en servicios externos.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Clearly define what is allowed and what is not; ask for declaration and citation of tools when appropriate; verify student understanding and contribution; avoid entering sensitive information into external services.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 'Lo que exige en la practica'
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim mirrors the guide's own practical bullet list and keeps the conditional 'when appropriate' language.

artifacts.claimText: The [genIA] teacher annex presents three orienting institutional template options for course or assignment instructions: generative AI allowed with conditions, restricted by stages, or prohibited except with explicit authorization.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Se contemplan tres opciones de lineamientos institucionales orientadores para explicitar el uso ético y responsable de herramientas de IA generativa en la asignatura: 1.1 Uso de tecnologías digitales emergentes e IA generativa permitido con condiciones. 1.2 Uso restringido de tecnologías emergentes e IA por etapas. 1.3 Uso prohibido (salvo autorización explícita) de tecnologías digitales emergentes.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The page lists three orienting institutional options for courses: AI use allowed with conditions, restricted by stages, or prohibited except with explicit authorization.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as guidance-template evidence only; the claim does not assert a single default rule for all UdeC courses.

artifacts.claimText: The [genIA] teacher annex suggests including a 'Declaracion de autoria humana y uso de tecnologias digitales emergentes como la IA Generativa' section in each assignment or academic activity submission.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Se sugiere que, en cada entrega de tareas, trabajos u otras actividades académicas, se incluya el apartado denominado: "Declaración de autoría humana y uso de tecnologías digitales emergentes como la IA Generativa".
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The page suggests including a human authorship and generative AI use declaration section in each assignment, work, or other academic activity submission.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Anexo 2, 'Plantillas de declaracion sobre autoria humana y uso de tecnologias digitales emergentes'
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved with 'suggests' wording because the original Spanish uses 'Se sugiere'.

artifacts.claimText: [genIA] offers formative spaces for the UdeC university community, including basic and intermediate workshops on generative AI tools in teaching with objectives covering ethics, academic integrity, advanced tool use, and educational-material design.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Este taller está dirigido a docentes sin conocimientos previos en inteligencia artificial generativa. Su propósito es introducir el uso cotidiano de estas tecnologías, resguardando la ética y la privacidad.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The basic workshop is for teachers without prior generative AI knowledge and introduces everyday use while safeguarding ethics and privacy.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 'Herramientas de IA generativa en la docencia', nivel basico
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Aplicar herramientas avanzadas de IAG para la optimización de la labor docente. Temas: Gestión avanzada de información, creación visual y representación del conocimiento, y diseño práctico de materiales educativos interactivos.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The intermediate course objective is to apply advanced generative AI tools to optimize teaching work, including information management and educational material design.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 'Herramientas de IA generativa en la docencia', nivel medio
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a training/offering claim rather than a student conduct rule.

artifacts.claimText: UdeC describes [genIA] as a transversal interdisciplinary AI collaboration program that articulates, orients, and makes visible how AI is learned, discussed, developed, and used responsibly in university life and in relation to society.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: El programa interdisciplinario de colaboración en Inteligencia Artificial [genIA] es una comunidad transversal de la Universidad de Concepción que articula, orienta y visibiliza cómo la inteligencia artificial se aprende, se discute, se desarrolla y se usa de manera responsable en la vida universitaria y en relación con la sociedad.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The page describes [genIA] as a transversal Universidad de Concepcion community that articulates how AI is learned, discussed, developed, and used responsibly in university life and society.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status/program characterization claim, not as a binding AI-use policy.

artifacts.claimText: The [genIA] technology line says the university community incorporates ethical impact evaluation to review proportionality, transparency, and risks before any AI deployment.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Evaluar riesgos, transparencia y proporcionalidad antes de cualquier despliegue de IA.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Evaluate risks, transparency, and proportionality before any AI deployment.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a program technology-line priority because the evidence directly states evaluation before deployment.

artifacts.title: Universidad de Concepcion AI policy and guidance staged review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official UdeC [genIA] institutional guidance on ethical and responsible generative AI use, including teaching/research guidance, practical declaration and privacy expectations, course-template options for AI use, training offerings, and technology-line risk/transparency evaluation priorities. Claims are agent-reviewed candidates only and are not marked canonical.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims summarize official [genIA] guidance pages and do not infer a binding disciplinary rule beyond the wording of those pages.
Faculty-only, repository/research, and third-party media sources were rejected for university-wide policy claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
